p0_manifest.text:
---
name: Jupyter (Example)
category: Interactive Apps
subcategory: Servers
role: batch_connect
description: |
  Example Jupyter launcher app, part of the Appverse Example Collection.
  This is a reference implementation — not deployable as-is.

p0_appverse.text:
# Per-app appverse.yml — Jupyter example
#
# This file enriches the catalog entry for the app at this subpath. The
# Collection-level appverse.yml at the repo root declares this subpath in
# its `apps:` list; the catalog reads this file to populate the app's
# catalog metadata.

software: Jupyter

name: Jupyter (Example)
description: A reference Jupyter launcher demonstrating per-app appverse.yml structure. Not deployable as-is.
app_type: Batch Connect

maintainer:
  name: ACCESS Support
  support_url: https://github.com/Sweet-and-Fizzy/appverse-example-collection/issues

tags:
  - Batch Connect
  - Containerized

p0_readme.text:
# Jupyter (Example)

Example Jupyter launcher app, part of the [Appverse Example Collection](../README.md). This is a reference implementation demonstrating how to structure a Batch Connect app within a multi-app repo. **Not deployable as-is** — the cluster name, module loads, job template, and other site-specific details are placeholders.

## Files in this directory

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| [`manifest.yml`](./manifest.yml) | Required by Open OnDemand to recognize the app. Supplies name, category, role, description. |
| [`form.yml`](./form.yml) | The launcher form users fill out. Defines `bc_num_hours` and `num_cores` fields. |
| [`appverse.yml`](./appverse.yml) | Per-app catalog metadata for the Appverse. Supplies name, description, app type, maintainer, tags. |

## What's missing for a real deployment

To make this app deployable, you'd add:

- `submit.yml.erb` — job submission configuration (template, queue, account, resource requests)
- `template/` directory with `script.sh.erb` and `before.sh.erb` — the job script and Jupyter launch logic
- `view.html.erb` — the connection page shown after the job starts
- Real cluster names in `form.yml`'s `cluster:` list (currently `example_cluster`)
- Module load commands or environment setup specific to your HPC site

See the [OSC bc_osc_jupyter](https://github.com/OSC/bc_osc_jupyter) repo for a complete real-world Jupyter app reference.

## License

Public domain — copy freely.

p0_form.text:
---
cluster:
  - "example_cluster"

form:
  - bc_num_hours
  - num_cores

attributes:
  bc_num_hours:
    widget: number_field
    min: 1
    max: 24
    step: 1
    value: 1
    label: Hours
  num_cores:
    widget: number_field
    min: 1
    max: 16
    step: 1
    value: 1
    label: Cores

p1_readme.text:
# RStudio (Example)

Example RStudio launcher app, part of the [Appverse Example Collection](../README.md). This is a reference implementation demonstrating how to structure a Batch Connect app within a multi-app repo. **Not deployable as-is** — the cluster name, module loads, job template, and other site-specific details are placeholders.

## Files in this directory

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| [`manifest.yml`](./manifest.yml) | Required by Open OnDemand to recognize the app. Supplies name, category, role, description. |
| [`form.yml`](./form.yml) | The launcher form users fill out. Defines `bc_num_hours` and `num_cores` fields. |
| [`appverse.yml`](./appverse.yml) | Per-app catalog metadata for the Appverse. Supplies name, description, app type, maintainer, tags. |

## What's missing for a real deployment

To make this app deployable, you'd add:

- `submit.yml.erb` — job submission configuration (template, queue, account, resource requests)
- `template/` directory with `script.sh.erb` and `before.sh.erb` — the job script and RStudio launch logic
- `view.html.erb` — the connection page shown after the job starts
- Real cluster names in `form.yml`'s `cluster:` list (currently `example_cluster`)
- Module load commands or environment setup specific to your HPC site

See the [OSC bc_osc_rstudio_server](https://github.com/OSC/bc_osc_rstudio_server) repo for a complete real-world RStudio app reference.

## License

Public domain — copy freely.

    step: 1

    label: Cores
